checks.name: levels are 31 -> 15 -> 7 -> 3 -> 1 (all odd, standard h -> 2h) and the 1x1 coarsest operator is exactly [[16]] = 4/h^2 at h = 1/2
rod rhs at n = 31: hot +1 col 4 rows 3-8, cold -1 col 26 rows 22-27 (0-based) -- the exact point-symmetric image of report 11's n = 32 rods: b + rot180(b) == 0, and so is the solution
per-mode smoothing factor identity: 1 - omega(sin^2 + sin^2) == 1 - omega h^2 lam_kl / 4 == report 12's 1 - omega lambda_k in D = 4/h^2 scaling
2-D DST-I with scale 1/(2(n+1)) == projection onto orthonormal sine modes (the spectra used by panels S1/S7)
high/low frequency split: exactly 15^2 = 225 coarse-representable modes (max(k,l) <= 15) = the number of coarse unknowns; 736 rough
smoother identity: matrix form u + omega D^{-1}(f - A u) == explicit 5-point stencil loop, 3 sweeps, random u0/f
per-mode contraction measured == mu_kl^m for all 961 modes, 25 sweeps (DST-projected)

smoothing factor over the HF quadrant == 3/5 EXACTLY at omega = 4/5: sup |1 - omega s| = max(|1 - omega/2|, |1 - 2 omega|) = 3/5, attained at both ends theta = (pi/2, 0) and (pi, pi); 1201^2 symbol grid never exceeds
omega = 4/5 is the HF-optimal damping (Trottenberg, report 12): argmin over omega of the HF sup is 0.7999 with value 0.6001; exact solution of |1 - omega/2| = |1 - 2 omega| is omega = 4/5, value 3/5

discrete smoothing factor at n = 31: max |mu| over rough modes = 0.598074 at mode (1, 16), within 0.4% of 3/5 (from below -- the discrete grid sits strictly inside the HF quadrant); empirical fit from rough-only decay, sweeps 10 -> 25: 0.598074
S1 smooth corner FROZEN: mode (1,1) keeps 0.6798 of its amplitude after 100 sweeps == mu_11^100 = 0.6798 (mu_11 = 0.996148)
S1 rough corner DEAD: max rough-mode amplitude after 100 sweeps is 1.55e-16 of its start (rounding floor; exact factor mu_disc^100 ~ 4e-23); rough ENERGY fraction 0.398 -> 2.95e-32

S1 stall: ||e|| drops only 4.84x in 100 sweeps (0.01795 -> 0.003705, under one order of magnitude); per-sweep ratio over sweeps 90-100 is 0.990050 -- inside the smooth-corner band [mu_22, mu_11] = [0.9846, 0.9961] and closest to mu_12 = 0.9904: the rod solution is point-antisymmetric so its slowest mode is (1,2) (report 13's parity note); only the noise part of x0 populates (1,1)

bilinear prolongation == 4 x full-weighting^T EXACTLY (2-D; the constant is 2^d: R rows sum to 1 -- an average -- while P columns sum to 4 -- a partition of unity spread over ~4 fine nodes; in the A = stencil/h^2 scaling this is what makes R A P a 2h-scale operator)
full weighting preserves constants: every R row sums to 1 (all 225 rows, including boundary-adjacent ones); every P column sums to 4
S3 smoothed error IS coarse-representable: decimate-then-interpolate loses 5.9% of the 5-sweep error and 2.7% of the 20-sweep error (rel L2)
S3 a rough field is NOT: same round trip loses 101% of a pure rough-mode field (>= 15x the smoothed error's loss); best-in-range(P) errors 0.971 vs 0.046
S3 representation error falls monotonically with smoothing sweeps 0.7448 > 0.0588 > 0.0266 > 0.0163
S8 trace has the full V shape: init, then (pre-smooth, restrict) at levels 31/15/7/3, division at 1, then (correct, post-smooth) at 3/7/15/31 -- 18 stages
every exported stage field is finite and consistent with its recorded norms (all 18 stages recomputed)
S4 full weighting: fine 31x31 residual (norm 9.741) -> coarse 15x15 rhs (norm 1.582); coarse rhs == R r exactly, quarter the unknowns at every level (225/961, 49/225, 9/49, 1/9)
S5 recursion bottoms out at 1 unknown: the exact solve is f/16 (f = 8.50722e-05 -> u = 5.31701e-06); residual and level error are exactly 0 there
S6 the big drop: adding the interpolated coarse solution cuts the fine error 4.1x in one shot (||e|| 0.01258 -> 0.003058)
S7 interpolation ripple killed: rough-mode energy fraction of the error 0.270 after correction -> 0.0289 after 2 post-sweeps (rough amplitudes cut ~mu_disc^2 = 0.358 per mode)
one V-cycle == one application of M^{-1}: vcycle(x0, b) == x0 + vcycle(0, b - A x0) to machine precision (linearity of every stage)
Galerkin operator R A P is symmetric (R = P^T/4) and positive definite
Galerkin interior stencil is translation-invariant and 9-point: H^2 * stencil == [[-1/4,-1/2,-1/4],[-1/2,3,-1/2],[-1/4,-1/2,-1/4]] (vs rediscretization's 5-point [[0,-1,0],[-1,4,-1],[0,-1,0]])

Galerkin != rediscretization, quantified honestly: rel Frobenius difference 0.330 (9-point vs 5-point), but both are O(h^2) discretizations -- they agree on smooth modes: relative difference of Rayleigh quotients on the 3 lowest coarse sine modes < 2%

two-grid rho at n = 31 (nu = 1+1, Galerkin coarse solve) = 0.3577 == report 12's mesh-independent house number 0.357 (same error propagator: full-weighting/bilinear Galerkin correction is the same A-orthogonal projection as report 12's bilinear coarse space)
two-grid rho with REDISCRETIZED coarse operator = 0.3577 -- within 15% of Galerkin's; rediscretization is what the stepper runs

WHY 0.357: both two-grid radii equal the DISCRETE SMOOTHING FACTOR SQUARED, mu_disc^2 = 0.3576924 (= 0.598074^2; one pre- + one post-sweep on the worst rough mode, which every coarse correction leaves untouched) -- Galerkin and rediscretized agree to machine precision because the binding mode is pure high-frequency

V(2,2)-cycle fitted rates agree with the exact spectral radius of E = I - M^{-1}A at every size (fit within [0.8, 1.05] x rho_exact: the fit is a lower-bound-ish transient average of |eigenvalues| <= rho)

MESH INDEPENDENCE: rho_V = 0.1872, 0.1901, 0.1908 at n = 15/31/63 -- spread 0.0036 (< 0.01), all bounded away from 1 (< 0.25): the same 14 cycles reach 1e-10 at every size, while Jacobi's rho crosses 0.999
damped-Jacobi spectral radius == 1 - 2 omega sin^2(pi h/2) at all three sizes (eigsh on the symmetric S confirms the smooth-end formula)
Jacobi rho -> 1 like 1 - O(h^2): gap ratios 3.990, 3.998 == 4 (h halving) within 3%; rho_J(63) = 0.999036 needs 23,884 sweeps to 1e-10 where the V-cycle needs 14 cycles

the V(2,2)-cycle from zero guess is a valid SPD preconditioner: dense M^{-1} at n=15 symmetric (rel dev 1.54e-16) with lam_min = 4.354e-04 > 0; adjointness dot-tests at n=31/63 pass (equal pre/post sweeps of the symmetric Jacobi smoother + P = 4 R^T)
V-PCG is mesh-independent: 8/8/8 iterations at n = 15/31/63 (spread <= 2) while plain CG grows ~2x per refinement: 35/74/149
geometric-series overhead: sum of level unknowns / N_fine = 1.2622 / 1.2955 / 1.3137 at n = 15/31/63 -- increasing toward and bounded by 4/3 (2-D quartering)

O(N) per cycle: flops/unknown = 85.5 / 91.2 / 94.2 at n = 15/31/63 (a 10% total rise across an 18x size range, settling: within 4% for 63-vs-31); whole-cycle overhead vs fine-level-only work 1.306 < 4/3

meta.rod_map_rule: n=32 rod index i -> floor((i+1)(n+1)/33 + 1/2) - 1, rows filled
meta.x0: standard_normal(seed 0), scaled to ||x*|| (half smooth, half rough)
meta.smoother: damped Jacobi, S = I - omega D^{-1} A, D = 4/h^2 I
meta.coarse_operators: rediscretized poisson_2d at every level (Galerkin R A P quantified in 'galerkin')
meta.spectra_convention: abs of orthonormal 2-D DST-I coefficients, 31x31, mode (k,l) at index [k-1][l-1]

s2_smoothing.mu_formula: 1 - omega (sin^2(k pi h/2) + sin^2(l pi h/2))

s3_representable.note: decimate = injection at coarse nodes (fine [1::2,1::2]); interpolate = bilinear P; errors are rel L2

s8_vcycle_trace.note: one V(2,2)-cycle at n=31 from x0; field_error = level-exact solution minus current iterate; fine_err_norm only at level-0 stages (coarse work does not change the fine iterate)

work.convention: report 11 Part C / report 14 SS4: 1 MAC = 2 flops, A-matvec = 2 nnz(A); sweep = matvec + N (f-Au) + 2N (axpy); residual = matvec + N; restriction 2 nnz(R); prolong-add 2 nnz(P) + N; coarsest = 1 divide
